decision: Zero-knowledge encryption — server stores only encrypted blobs
reason: Core privacy differentiator; server compromise does not expose user health data

decision: ShareView is a separate collection from Share.viewLog (both maintained)
reason: Share.viewLog is an embedded array on the Share document — fine for small counts. ShareView is a separate TTL collection for long-term audit queries without loading the entire Share doc. Both are kept to avoid breaking the existing viewLog consumers.

decision: SHA-256 full hash stored in ShareView.recipientIp (not truncated)
reason: Full SHA-256 allows correct uniqueViewers counting via distinct(). The Share.viewLog keeps the existing truncated 16-char hex for backwards compatibility. Raw IP is never persisted.

decision: ShareView.create is fire-and-forget (not awaited in public route)
reason: Audit logging must not add latency to the share access response path. Errors are caught and logged to console but do not degrade the recipient experience.
